examples.title: Trophic Level Energy Transfer Simulation
examples.description: Simulates energy transfer between trophic levels in a marine ecosystem
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the 10% energy transfer rule in marine food chains. Starting with 10,000 kcal of phytoplankton energy, only about 10 kcal reaches apex predators, showing why there are fewer organisms at higher trophic levels.

examples.title: Ocean Depth Pressure Calculator
examples.description: Calculates approximate pressure at different ocean depths
examples.code:
// Ocean Depth Pressure Calculation
// Pressure increases approximately 1 atm per 10 meters

CONSTANT PRESSURE_PER_10M = 1.0 // atmospheres
CONSTANT SURFACE_PRESSURE = 1.0 // atm
CONSTANT ATMOSPHERE_TO_KPA = 101.325

FUNCTION calculatePressure(depthInMeters):
 // Pressure in atmospheres
 pressureATM = SURFACE_PRESSURE + (depthInMeters / 10.0)
 
 // Convert to kilopascals
 pressureKPA = pressureATM * ATMOSPHERE_TO_KPA
 
 RETURN pressureATM, pressureKPA

FUNCTION classifyOceanZone(depth):
 IF depth <= 200:
 RETURN "Epipelagic Zone (Sunlight Zone)"
 ELSE IF depth <= 1000:
 RETURN "Mesopelagic Zone (Twilight Zone)"
 ELSE IF depth <= 4000:
 RETURN "Bathypelagic Zone (Midnight Zone)"
 ELSE IF depth <= 6000:
 RETURN "Abyssopelagic Zone (Abyssal Zone)"
 ELSE:
 RETURN "Hadopelagic Zone (Hadal Zone)"

// Example calculations
PRINT "At 200m depth: " + calculatePressure(200)
PRINT "At 1000m depth: " + calculatePressure(1000)
PRINT "At 4000m depth: " + calculatePressure(4000)
PRINT classifyOceanZone(3500)
examples.explanation: This program calculates ocean pressure at different depths and classifies which zone a given depth belongs to. Pressure increases by about 1 atmosphere for every 10 meters of depth, which is important for understanding the challenges marine organisms face at different depths.
